name: Iterative BST Walk
summary: Use the BST ordering: at each node, if both p and q are smaller go left, if both are larger go right; the first node where they split is the LCA.

lineExplanations.3: Read how many slots the level-order array has.
lineExplanations.4: nodes will hold one dict per real (non-null) tree node — this is what the stage draws.
lineExplanations.5: by_id maps a node id to its dict, so we can jump to any node in O(1).
lineExplanations.6: state_by_id[i] tracks how the stage should colour node i (0 idle, 1 current, 2 already passed).
lineExplanations.7: Give every slot a starting colour state of 0 (idle).
lineExplanations.8: Append one idle-state entry per slot.
lineExplanations.9: child walks the array to hand out left/right children in level order.
lineExplanations.10: Rebuild the tree: visit each array slot in order.
lineExplanations.11: Only real values become nodes; null slots are skipped.
lineExplanations.12: Create this node's dict with its id, value, and empty child links.
lineExplanations.13: If there is still an array slot left, it is this node's left child.
lineExplanations.14: A non-null slot means a real left child exists.
lineExplanations.15: Link the left child by its array index.
lineExplanations.16: Advance the child walker past the left slot.
lineExplanations.17: If another slot remains, it is this node's right child.
lineExplanations.18: A non-null slot means a real right child exists.
lineExplanations.19: Link the right child by its array index.
lineExplanations.20: Advance the child walker past the right slot.
lineExplanations.21: Add the finished node to the drawn node list.
lineExplanations.22: Register the node in by_id for O(1) lookups.
lineExplanations.23: Start the walk at the root (node id 0).
lineExplanations.24: answer will hold the LCA value; -1 is a placeholder until we find it.
lineExplanations.25: found flips to true the moment we reach the split node.
lineExplanations.26: Keep walking while we still have a node to inspect.
lineExplanations.27: Read the value stored at the current node.
lineExplanations.28: Mark the current node as the one we are inspecting right now.
lineExplanations.29: If BOTH p and q are smaller than this node, the LCA must be in the left subtree.
lineExplanations.30: We are leaving this node — mark it as already passed.
lineExplanations.31: Step down to the left child.
lineExplanations.32: If BOTH p and q are larger, the LCA must be in the right subtree.
lineExplanations.33: We are leaving this node — mark it as already passed.
lineExplanations.34: Step down to the right child.
lineExplanations.36: Otherwise p and q sit on opposite sides (or one equals this node): this node is the LCA.
lineExplanations.37: Record that the LCA has been found.
lineExplanations.38: Stop walking — the answer is fixed.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — LeetCode's standard wrapper class for your solution.
syntaxExplanations.2: def lowestCommonAncestor(self, values, p, q): — values is the level-order tree; p and q are the two target node values.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(values) counts the array slots (including nulls).
syntaxExplanations.5: {} creates an empty dictionary (a hash map).
syntaxExplanations.6: Another empty list, used to hold per-node colour states.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(n): loops i from 0 to n-1.
syntaxExplanations.8: .append(0) adds a 0 to the end of the list.
syntaxExplanations.9: child = 1 — the root sits at slot 0, so its children start at slot 1.
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n): a second pass, this time building the nodes.
syntaxExplanations.11: is not None checks that a slot holds a real value rather than a gap.
syntaxExplanations.12: A dict literal { } stores this node's fields; None means 'no child yet'.
syntaxExplanations.13: child < n guards against reading past the end of the array.
syntaxExplanations.14: Check whether the left-child slot holds a real value.
syntaxExplanations.15: node["left"] = child stores the left child's array index.
syntaxExplanations.16: child = child + 1 moves the walker to the next slot.
syntaxExplanations.17: Again check there is a slot left for the right child.
syntaxExplanations.18: Check whether the right-child slot holds a real value.
syntaxExplanations.19: node["right"] = child stores the right child's array index.
syntaxExplanations.20: Advance the walker past the right slot.
syntaxExplanations.21: nodes.append(node) adds this node to the drawn list.
syntaxExplanations.22: by_id[i] = node keys the node by its id for fast lookup.
syntaxExplanations.23: 0 if n > 0 else None — a ternary: root id 0 when the tree is non-empty.
syntaxExplanations.24: answer = -1 — a placeholder that will be overwritten.
syntaxExplanations.25: found = False — a boolean flag, starts false.
syntaxExplanations.26: while current is not None: keep looping until we run out of nodes.
syntaxExplanations.27: by_id[current]["value"] reads the current node's stored value.
syntaxExplanations.28: state_by_id[current] = 1 marks this node 'current' for the animation.
syntaxExplanations.29: and requires BOTH comparisons to be true.
syntaxExplanations.31: by_id[current]["left"] is the left child's id (or None).
syntaxExplanations.32: elif is checked only when the first if was false.
syntaxExplanations.33: Mark this node 'passed' before moving right.
syntaxExplanations.34: by_id[current]["right"] is the right child's id (or None).
syntaxExplanations.35: else: the remaining case — p and q are on opposite sides.
syntaxExplanations.36: answer = current_val stores this node's value as the LCA.
syntaxExplanations.37: found = True records that we located the answer.
syntaxExplanations.38: break exits the while loop immediately.
syntaxExplanations.39: return answer hands the LCA value back.

visualizationIntent: init: rebuild and show the whole BST, cursor about to start at the root. loop: highlight the current node as the cursor. check: compare p and q against the current node's value. move: mark the current node passed and glide the cursor to the left or right child. return: flash the split node gold as the LCA.
